$comment: D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS (c72, 2026-07-01): unary `~` (BitNot) and `-` (Neg) on a SUB-INT operand must integer-PROMOTE the operand to `int` (C 6.5.3.3p3/p4) BEFORE the MIR Neg/Not, and carry the PROMOTED result type. c71 promoted the condition / ++-- / `!` sites (combineUnaryOp for `!` only); the post-c71 sqlite3.c re-probe cleared the 902 Char errors and left 8 on U8/U16 (ordinal 6/7) at a '<deferred>' opcode = `~u8`/`-u8`/`~u16` (sqlite's u8/u16 flag math like `x & ~mask`). Binary `&`/`|`/`^`/`<<` already promote via combineBinary; only the UNARY `~`/`-` bypassed it (combineUnaryOp built a sub-int-typed UnaryOp Not/Neg with result=operand.type). FIX: generalize c71's combineUnaryOp promotion from Not to {Not, BitNot, Neg} — coerce the operand to integerPromotedType (u8/u16->ZExt, signed char->SExt) and, for `~`/`-`, set the RESULT type to the promoted type (for `!` it stays Bool); Bool excluded (not gated). This corpus: (u8)~(u8)0xF0=15 + (~(u16)0xFFF0 & 0xFF)=15 + (-(u8)1 == -1 -> +5) + (~(signed char)0 == -1 -> +7) => 42. Every observable is masked to the low byte (signedness-agnostic) or uses `signed char` explicitly, so it matches gcc on all 4 legs (DSS types char signed uniformly; gcc uses unsigned char on arm64). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the BitNot/Neg promotion -> error[L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode] '<deferred>: integer TypeKind ordinal 6/7 has no native-width ALU forms' (a compile failure, not 42). gcc/clang compile it and exit 42. Sibling of the c71 corpus char_alu_integer_promotion (same anchor, the unary operators the c71 audit flagged as latent).
